```text
   110→// Check verifies OpenAI Codex CLI installation and returns version info.
   111→func (p *Provider) Check(customCommand string) spi.CheckResult {
   112→	codexCmd, _ := parseCodexCommand(customCommand)
   113→	isCustomCommand := customCommand != ""
   114→
   115→	resolvedPath := codexCmd
   116→	if !filepath.IsAbs(codexCmd) {
   117→		if path, err := exec.LookPath(codexCmd); err == nil {
   118→			resolvedPath = path
   119→		}
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	versionOutput, versionFlag, stderrOutput, err := runCodexVersionCommand(codexCmd)
   123→	if err != nil {
   124→		errorType := classifyCheckError(err)
   125→		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, analytics.Properties{
   126→			"provider":            "codex",
   127→			"custom_command":      isCustomCommand,
   128→			"command_path":        codexCmd,
   129→			"resolved_path":       resolvedPath,
   130→			"error_type":          errorType,
   131→			"version_flag":        versionFlag,
   132→			"stderr":              stderrOutput,
   133→			"error_message":       err.Error(),
   134→			"path_classification": classifyCodexPath(codexCmd, resolvedPath),
   135→		})
   136→
   137→		errorMessage := buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType, codexCmd, isCustomCommand, stderrOutput)
   138→
   139→		return spi.CheckResult{
   140→			Success:      false,
   141→			Version:      "",
   142→			Location:     resolvedPath,
   143→			ErrorMessage: errorMessage,
   144→		}
   145→	}
   146→
   147→	if versionOutput == "" {
   148→		errorType := "no_output"
   149→		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, analytics.Properties{
   150→			"provider":            "codex",
   151→			"custom_command":      isCustomCommand,
   152→			"command_path":        codexCmd,
   153→			"resolved_path":       resolvedPath,
   154→			"error_type":          errorType,
   155→			"version_flag":        versionFlag,
   156→			"stderr":              stderrOutput,
   157→			"path_classification": classifyCodexPath(codexCmd, resolvedPath),
   158→		})
   159→
   160→		errorMessage := buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType, codexCmd, isCustomCommand, stderrOutput)
   161→
   162→		return spi.CheckResult{
   163→			Success:      false,
   164→			Version:      "",
   165→			Location:     resolvedPath,
   166→			ErrorMessage: errorMessage,
   167→		}
   168→	}
   169→
   170→	pathType := classifyCodexPath(codexCmd, resolvedPath)
   171→	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallSuccess, analytics.Properties{
   172→		"provider":       "codex",
   173→		"custom_command": isCustomCommand,
   174→		"command_path":   resolvedPath,
   175→		"path_type":      pathType,
   176→		"version":        versionOutput,
   177→		"version_flag":   versionFlag,
   178→	})
   179→
   180→	slog.Debug("Codex CLI check successful", "version", versionOutput, "location", resolvedPath, "flag", versionFlag)
   181→
   182→	return spi.CheckResult{
   183→		Success:      true,
   184→		Version:      versionOutput,
   185→		Location:     resolvedPath,
   186→		ErrorMessage: "",
   187→	}
   188→}
   189→
   190→// DetectAgent checks if OpenAI Codex CLI has been used in the given project path.
   191→func (p *Provider) DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool {
   192→	// Try to find sessions using the shared helper (stop on first match)
   193→	sessions, err := findCodexSessions(projectPath, "", true)
   194→
   195→	// Handle different error cases with helpful output
   196→	if err != nil {
   197→		// Check if it's a home directory error
   198→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "home directory") {
   199→			slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Failed to resolve user home directory", "error", err)
   200→			if helpOutput {
   201→				fmt.Println()
   202→				log.UserWarn("Could not scan Codex CLI sessions for this directory.\n")
   203→				log.UserMessage("Reason: failed to determine your home directory.\n")
   204→				fmt.Println()
   205→			}
   206→			return false
   207→		}
   208→
   209→		// Check if it's a sessions directory error
   210→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions directory not accessible") || strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions root") {
   211→			homeDir, _ := osUserHomeDir()
   212→			sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
   213→
   214→			slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Codex sessions directory not accessible", "error", err)
   215→			if helpOutput {
   216→				fmt.Println()
   217→				log.UserWarn("No Codex CLI sessions were found for this directory.\n")
   218→				log.UserMessage("Codex CLI stores activity under ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/. We couldn't find that directory.\n\n")
   219→				log.UserMessage("To fix this:\n")
   220→				log.UserMessage("  1. Run `specstory run codex` to launch Codex CLI in this project\n")
   221→				log.UserMessage("  2. Or start Codex CLI manually, then run `specstory sync codex` afterward\n\n")
   222→				log.UserMessage("Expected sessions directory: %s\n", sessionsRoot)
   223→				fmt.Println()
   224→			}
   225→			return false
   226→		}
   227→
   228→		// Unknown error
   229→		slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Error finding sessions", "error", err)
   230→		return false
   231→	}
   232→
   233→	// Check if we found any sessions
   234→	if len(sessions) > 0 {
   235→		session := sessions[0]
   236→		slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Codex CLI activity detected",
   237→			"sessionID", session.SessionID,
   238→			"sessionPath", session.SessionPath)
   239→		return true
   240→	}
   241→
   242→	// No sessions found - provide helpful output
   243→	if helpOutput {
   244→		homeDir, _ := osUserHomeDir()
   245→		sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
   246→
   247→		fmt.Println()
   248→		log.UserWarn("No Codex CLI sessions were found for this directory.\n")
   249→		log.UserMessage("Codex CLI hasn't saved a session with this working directory yet.\n")
   250→		log.UserMessage("Codex stores sessions in ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/ as JSONL files.\n\n")
   251→		log.UserMessage("To fix this:\n")
   252→		log.UserMessage("  1. Run `specstory run codex` to launch Codex CLI here\n")
   253→		log.UserMessage("  2. Or open Codex CLI manually in this project, then try syncing again\n\n")
   254→		log.UserMessage("Checked sessions directory: %s\n", sessionsRoot)
   255→		fmt.Println()
   256→	}
   257→
   258→	return false
   259→}
   260→
   261→// GetAgentChatSessions retrieves chat sessions for the given project path.
   262→func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
   263→	// Find all sessions for this project (don't stop on first)
   264→	sessions, err := findCodexSessions(projectPath, "", false)
   265→	if err != nil {
   266→		// If sessions directory doesn't exist, return empty list (not an error)
   267→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions directory not accessible") ||
   268→			strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions root") ||
   269→			strings.Contains(err.Error(), "home directory") {
   270→			return []spi.AgentChatSession{}, nil
   271→		}
   272→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to find codex sessions: %w", err)
   273→	}
   274→
   275→	totalSessions := len(sessions)
   276→
   277→	// Convert to AgentChatSession structs with progress reporting
   278→	var result []spi.AgentChatSession
   279→	for i, sessionInfo := range sessions {
   280→		session, err := processSessionToAgentChat(&sessionInfo, projectPath, debugRaw)
   281→		if err != nil {
   282→			slog.Debug("GetAgentChatSessions: Failed to process session",
   283→				"sessionID", sessionInfo.SessionID,
   284→				"error", err)
   285→			// Report progress even for failed sessions
   286→			if progress != nil {
   287→				progress(i+1, totalSessions)
   288→			}
   289→			continue // Skip sessions we can't process
   290→		}
   291→
   292→		// Skip empty sessions (processSessionToAgentChat returns nil for empty sessions)
   293→		if session == nil {
   294→			slog.Debug("GetAgentChatSessions: Skipping empty session",
   295→				"sessionID", sessionInfo.SessionID)
   296→			// Report progress even for empty sessions
   297→			if progress != nil {
   298→				progress(i+1, totalSessions)
   299→			}
   300→			continue
   301→		}
   302→
   303→		result = append(result, *session)
   304→
   305→		// Report progress after each session
   306→		if progress != nil {
   307→			progress(i+1, totalSessions)
   308→		}
   309→	}
   310→
   311→	return result, nil
   312→}
   313→
   314→// GetAgentChatSession retrieves a chat session by ID.
   315→func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
   316→	// Find the specific session (will short-circuit when found)
   317→	sessions, err := findCodexSessions(projectPath, sessionID, false)
   318→	if err != nil {
   319→		// If sessions directory doesn't exist, return nil (not an error)
   320→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions directory not accessible") ||
   321→			strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions root") ||
   322→			strings.Contains(err.Error(), "home directory") {
   323→			return nil, nil
   324→		}
   325→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to find codex sessions: %w", err)
   326→	}
   327→
   328→	// Session not found (empty result)
   329→	if len(sessions) == 0 {
   330→		return nil, nil
   331→	}
   332→
   333→	// Process the first (and only) session using shared helper
   334→	return processSessionToAgentChat(&sessions[0], projectPath, debugRaw)
   335→}
   336→
   337→// ExecAgentAndWatch executes the Codex CLI in interactive mode and monitors for session updates.
   338→// The function blocks until the Codex CLI exits. During execution, it watches for JSONL file changes
   339→// and invokes sessionCallback for each update, enabling real-time markdown generation and cloud sync.
   340→func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
   341→	slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: Starting Codex CLI execution and monitoring",
   342→		"projectPath", projectPath,
   343→		"customCommand", customCommand,
   344→		"resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID,
   345→		"debugRaw", debugRaw)
   346→
   347→	// Log if resuming a specific session
   348→	if resumeSessionID != "" {
   349→		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: Will resume specific Codex session", "sessionID", resumeSessionID)
   350→	}
   351→
   352→	// Set up the callback which enables real-time markdown generation and cloud sync during
   353→	// interactive sessions. As Codex CLI writes JSONL updates, the watcher detects changes
   354→	// and invokes this callback, allowing immediate processing without blocking the agent's
   355→	// execution. The defer ensures cleanup when the agent exits.
   356→	SetWatcherCallback(sessionCallback)
   357→	defer ClearWatcherCallback()
   358→
   359→	// Set debug raw mode for the watcher
   360→	SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw)
   361→
   362→	// Start watching for Codex sessions in the background
   363→	slog.Info("Initializing Codex session monitoring...")
   364→
   365→	if err := WatchForCodexSessions(projectPath, resumeSessionID); err != nil {
   366→		// Log the error but don't fail - watcher might work later
   367→		slog.Error("Failed to start Codex session watcher", "error", err)
   368→	}
   369→
   370→	// Execute Codex CLI - this blocks until Codex exits
   371→	slog.Info("Executing Codex CLI", "command", customCommand, "resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID)
   372→	err := ExecuteCodex(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
   373→
   374→	// Stop the watcher goroutine and wait for it to finish before returning
   375→	slog.Info("Codex CLI has exited, stopping watcher")
   376→	StopWatcher()
   377→
   378→	// Return any execution error
   379→	if err != nil {
   380→		return fmt.Errorf("codex CLI execution failed: %w", err)
   381→	}
   382→
   383→	return nil
   384→}
   385→
   386→// WatchAgent watches for Codex CLI agent activity and calls the callback with AgentChatSession
   387→// Does NOT execute the agent - only watches for existing activity
   388→// Runs until error or context cancellation (blocks indefinitely)
   389→func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
   390→	slog.Info("WatchAgent: Starting Codex CLI activity monitoring",
   391→		"projectPath", projectPath,
   392→		"debugRaw", debugRaw)
   393→
   394→	// The watcher callback directly passes AgentChatSession (which now contains SessionData)
   395→	wrappedCallback := func(agentChatSession *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   396→		slog.Debug("WatchAgent: Received AgentChatSession",
   397→			"sessionID", agentChatSession.SessionID,
   398→			"hasSessionData", agentChatSession.SessionData != nil)
   399→
   400→		// Call the user's callback with the AgentChatSession
   401→		sessionCallback(agentChatSession)
   402→	}
   403→
   404→	// Set up the callback for the watcher
   405→	SetWatcherCallback(wrappedCallback)
   406→	defer ClearWatcherCallback()
   407→
   408→	// Set debug raw mode for the watcher
   409→	SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw)
   410→
   411→	// Start watching for Codex sessions in the background
   412→	slog.Info("WatchAgent: Initializing Codex session monitoring...")
   413→
   414→	if err := WatchForCodexSessions(projectPath, ""); err != nil {
   415→		slog.Error("WatchAgent: Failed to start Codex session watcher", "error", err)
   416→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to start watcher: %w", err)
   417→	}
   418→
   419→	// Block until context is cancelled
   420→	// The watcher runs in a background goroutine and will continue
   421→	// until context is cancelled or the process exits
   422→	slog.Info("WatchAgent: Watcher started, blocking until context cancelled")
   423→
   424→	// Wait for context cancellation
   425→	<-ctx.Done()
   426→
   427→	slog.Info("WatchAgent: Context cancelled, stopping watcher")
   428→	StopWatcher()
   429→
   430→	return ctx.Err()
   431→}
   432→
   433→// findCodexSessions traverses the Codex CLI sessions directory structure and finds
   434→// sessions that match the given project path.
   435→//
   436→// Short-circuit behavior:
   437→//   - If targetSessionID is provided: stops when that specific session is found (ignores stopOnFirst)
   438→//   - If targetSessionID is empty and stopOnFirst is true: stops after finding first matching session
   439→//   - If targetSessionID is empty and stopOnFirst is false: returns all matching sessions
   440→func findCodexSessions(projectPath string, targetSessionID string, stopOnFirst bool) ([]codexSessionInfo, error) {
   441→	normalizedProjectPath := normalizeCodexPath(projectPath)
   442→	if normalizedProjectPath == "" {
   443→		slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Unable to normalize project path", "projectPath", projectPath)
   444→	}
   445→
   446→	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
   447→	if err != nil {
   448→		slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Failed to resolve user home directory", "error", err)
   449→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to determine home directory: %w", err)
   450→	}
   451→
   452→	sessionsRoot := codexSessionsRoot(homeDir)
   453→	rootInfo, err := os.Stat(sessionsRoot)
   454→	if err != nil {
   455→		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
   456→			slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Codex sessions directory not found", "path", sessionsRoot)
   457→		} else {
   458→			slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Failed to stat Codex sessions directory", "path", sessionsRoot, "error", err)
   459→		}
   460→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("sessions directory not accessible: %w", err)
   461→	}
   462→
   463→	if !rootInfo.IsDir() {
   464→		slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Codex sessions root is not a directory", "path", sessionsRoot)
   465→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("sessions root is not a directory: %s", sessionsRoot)
   466→	}
   467→
   468→	yearEntries, err := readDirSortedDesc(sessionsRoot)
   469→	if err != nil {
   470→		slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Failed to read Codex sessions root", "path", sessionsRoot, "error", err)
   471→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read sessions root: %w", err)
   472→	}
   473→
   474→	var sessions []codexSessionInfo
   475→
   476→	// Traverse year/month/day directory structure
   477→	for _, yearEntry := range yearEntries {
   478→		if !yearEntry.IsDir() {
   479→			continue
   480→		}
   481→
   482→		yearPath := filepath.Join(sessionsRoot, yearEntry.Name())
   483→		monthEntries, err := readDirSortedDesc(yearPath)
   484→		if err != nil {
   485→			slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Failed to read Codex year directory", "path", yearPath, "error", err)
   486→			continue
   487→		}
   488→
   489→		for _, monthEntry := range monthEntries {
   490→			if !monthEntry.IsDir() {
   491→				continue
   492→			}
   493→
   494→			monthPath := filepath.Join(yearPath, monthEntry.Name())
   495→			dayEntries, err := readDirSortedDesc(monthPath)
   496→			if err != nil {
   497→				slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Failed to read Codex month directory", "path", monthPath, "error", err)
   498→				continue
   499→			}
   500→
   501→			for _, dayEntry := range dayEntries {
   502→				if !dayEntry.IsDir() {
   503→					continue
   504→				}
   505→
   506→				dayPath := filepath.Join(monthPath, dayEntry.Name())
   507→				sessionEntries, err := readDirSortedDesc(dayPath)
   508→				if err != nil {
   509→					slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Failed to read Codex day directory", "path", dayPath, "error", err)
   510→					continue
   511→				}
   512→
   513→				for _, sessionEntry := range sessionEntries {
   514→					if sessionEntry.IsDir() {
   515→						continue
   516→					}
   517→					if filepath.Ext(sessionEntry.Name()) != ".jsonl" {
   518→						continue
   519→					}
   520→
   521→					sessionPath := filepath.Join(dayPath, sessionEntry.Name())
   522→					meta, err := loadCodexSessionMeta(sessionPath)
   523→					if err != nil {
   524→						slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Failed to load Codex session meta", "path", sessionPath, "error", err)
   525→						continue
   526→					}
   527→
   528→					sessionID := strings.TrimSpace(meta.Payload.ID)
   529→					normalizedCWD := normalizeCodexPath(meta.Payload.CWD)
   530→					if normalizedCWD == "" {
   531→						slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Session meta missing cwd", "sessionID", sessionID, "path", sessionPath)
   532→						continue
   533→					}
   534→
   535→					// Check if this session matches the project path
   536→					matched := false
   537→					if normalizedProjectPath != "" {
   538→						if normalizedCWD == normalizedProjectPath || strings.EqualFold(normalizedCWD, normalizedProjectPath) {
   539→							matched = true
   540→						}
   541→					} else if normalizedCWD == projectPath || strings.EqualFold(normalizedCWD, projectPath) {
   542→						matched = true
   543→					}
   544→
   545→					if matched {
   546→						// If we're searching for a specific session, skip sessions that don't match
   547→						if targetSessionID != "" && sessionID != targetSessionID {
   548→							continue
   549→						}
   550→
   551→						slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Codex CLI session matched project",
   552→							"sessionID", sessionID,
   553→							"sessionPath", sessionPath)
   554→
   555→						sessions = append(sessions, codexSessionInfo{
   556→							SessionID:   sessionID,
   557→							SessionPath: sessionPath,
   558→							Meta:        meta,
   559→						})
   560→
   561→						// Short-circuit logic:
   562→						// 1. If we have a target sessionID and this matches, we're done
   563→						if targetSessionID != "" && sessionID == targetSessionID {
   564→							slog.Debug("findCodexSessions: Found target session, stopping search", "sessionID", targetSessionID)
   565→							return sessions, nil
   566→						}
   567→
   568→						// 2. If stopOnFirst is true (and no target sessionID), return first match
   569→						if targetSessionID == "" && stopOnFirst {
   570→							return sessions, nil
   571→						}
   572→
   573→						// 3. Otherwise, continue collecting all matching sessions
   574→					}
   575→				}
   576→			}
   577→		}
   578→	}
   579→
   580→	return sessions, nil
   581→}
   582→
   583→// readSessionRawData reads all JSONL lines from a Codex CLI session file and returns
   584→// both the parsed records and the raw JSONL content.
   585→func readSessionRawData(sessionPath string) ([]map[string]interface{}, string, error) {
   586→	file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
   587→	if err != nil {
   588→		return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
   589→	}
   590→	defer func() {
   591→		_ = file.Close()
   592→	}()
   593→
   594→	var records []map[string]interface{}
   595→	var rawBuilder strings.Builder
   596→
   597→	// Use bufio.Reader instead of Scanner to handle arbitrarily large lines
   598→	// Scanner has a token size limit (even with custom buffer), but Reader does not
   599→	reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
   600→
   601→	lineNumber := 0
   602→	for {
   603→		// Read line using ReadString which has no size limit
   604→		line, err := reader.ReadString('\n')
   605→		line = strings.TrimSuffix(line, "\n")
   606→
   607→		// EOF is expected at end of file, other errors are genuine failures
   608→		if err != nil && err != io.EOF {
   609→			return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("error reading line %d: %w", lineNumber+1, err)
   610→		}
   611→
   612→		// Determine if we're at end of file and if we have content to process
   613→		atEOF := err == io.EOF
   614→		hasContent := len(line) > 0
   615→
   616→		// Increment line number for every line read (including empty lines) to match text editor line numbers
   617→		if hasContent || !atEOF {
   618→			lineNumber++
   619→		}
   620→
   621→		// If no content, either skip empty line or exit at EOF
   622→		if !hasContent {
   623→			if atEOF {
   624→				break // Reached end of file with no content
   625→			}
   626→			continue // Empty line in middle of file, skip it
   627→		}
   628→
   629→		// Sanity check to prevent OOM from pathological files
   630→		if len(line) > maxReasonableLineSize {
   631→			slog.Warn("line exceeds reasonable size limit",
   632→				"lineNumber", lineNumber,
   633→				"sizeMB", len(line)/MB,
   634→				"limitMB", maxReasonableLineSize/MB,
   635→				"file", filepath.Base(sessionPath))
   636→			return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("line %d exceeds reasonable size limit (%d MB): refusing to process potentially malformed file",
   637→				lineNumber, maxReasonableLineSize/MB)
   638→		}
   639→
   640→		// Log when processing unusually large lines (helps debug performance issues)
   641→		if len(line) > 10*MB {
   642→			slog.Debug("processing large JSONL line",
   643→				"lineNumber", lineNumber,
   644→				"sizeMB", len(line)/MB,
   645→				"file", filepath.Base(sessionPath))
   646→		}
   647→
   648→		// Trim whitespace and skip empty lines
   649→		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   650→		if line == "" {
   651→			if atEOF {
   652→				break
   653→			}
   654→			continue
   655→		}
   656→
   657→		// Add to raw data
   658→		rawBuilder.WriteString(line)
   659→		rawBuilder.WriteString("\n")
   660→
   661→		// Parse JSON
   662→		var record map[string]interface{}
   663→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {
   664→			// Log warning and skip corrupted lines rather than failing entire parse
   665→			slog.Warn("Skipping corrupted JSONL line",
   666→				"file", filepath.Base(sessionPath),
   667→				"line", lineNumber,
   668→				"error", err)
   669→			if atEOF {
   670→				break
   671→			}
   672→			continue
   673→		}
   674→
   675→		records = append(records, record)
   676→
   677→		// After processing record, check if we're done
   678→		if atEOF {
   679→			break
   680→		}
   681→	}
   682→
   683→	return records, rawBuilder.String(), nil
   684→}
   685→
   686→// loadCodexSessionMeta reads the first JSON line from a session file and parses the session metadata.
   687→func loadCodexSessionMeta(sessionPath string) (*codexSessionMeta, error) {
   688→	file, err := os.Open(sessionPath)
   689→	if err != nil {
   690→		return nil, err
   691→	}
   692→	defer func() {
   693→		_ = file.Close()
   694→	}()
   695→
   696→	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(file)
   697→	if !scanner.Scan() {
   698→		if scanErr := scanner.Err(); scanErr != nil {
   699→			return nil, scanErr
   700→		}
   701→		return nil, errors.New("codex session meta not found")
   702→	}
   703→
   704→	line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
   705→	if line == "" {
   706→		return nil, errors.New("codex session meta is empty")
   707→	}
   708→
   709→	var meta codexSessionMeta
   710→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &meta); err != nil {
   711→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse codex session meta: %w", err)
   712→	}
   713→
   714→	if meta.Type != "session_meta" {
   715→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected codex session record type: %s", meta.Type)
   716→	}
   717→
   718→	return &meta, nil
   719→}
   720→
   721→// processSessionToAgentChat converts a codexSessionInfo into an AgentChatSession by reading
   722→// the session data, generating SessionData, and optionally writing debug files. Returns nil if
   723→// the session is empty or cannot be read.
   724→func processSessionToAgentChat(sessionInfo *codexSessionInfo, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
   725→	// Read the session data
   726→	records, rawData, err := readSessionRawData(sessionInfo.SessionPath)
   727→	if err != nil {
   728→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read session data: %w", err)
   729→	}
   730→
   731→	// Skip empty sessions
   732→	if len(records) == 0 {
   733→		return nil, nil
   734→	}
   735→
   736→	// Get timestamp from metadata
   737→	timestamp := sessionInfo.Meta.Timestamp
   738→
   739→	// Generate slug from first user message
   740→	firstUserMessage := findFirstUserMessage(records)
   741→	slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(firstUserMessage)
   742→	if slug == "" {
   743→		slog.Debug("processSessionToAgentChat: No user message for slug yet",
   744→			"sessionID", sessionInfo.SessionID)
   745→	} else {
   746→		slog.Debug("processSessionToAgentChat: Generated slug from user message",
   747→			"sessionID", sessionInfo.SessionID,
   748→			"slug", slug)
   749→	}
   750→
   751→	// Generate SessionData from records
   752→	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(records, workspaceRoot)
   753→	if err != nil {
   754→		slog.Error("Failed to generate SessionData", "sessionId", sessionInfo.SessionID, "error", err)
   755→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to generate SessionData: %w", err)
   756→	}
   757→
   758→	// Write provider-specific debug files if requested
   759→	if debugRaw {
   760→		if err := writeDebugRawFiles(sessionInfo.SessionID, records); err != nil {
   761→			slog.Debug("processSessionToAgentChat: Failed to write debug files",
   762→				"sessionID", sessionInfo.SessionID,
   763→				"error", err)
   764→			// Don't fail the operation if debug output fails
   765→		}
   766→	}
   767→
   768→	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
   769→		SessionID:   sessionInfo.SessionID,
   770→		CreatedAt:   timestamp,
   771→		Slug:        slug,
   772→		SessionData: sessionData,
   773→		RawData:     rawData,
   774→	}, nil
   775→}
   776→
   777→// writeDebugRawFiles writes debug JSON files for a Codex CLI session.
   778→// Each record is written as a numbered JSON file in .specstory/debug/<session-id>/
   779→func writeDebugRawFiles(sessionID string, records []map[string]interface{}) error {
   780→	// Get the debug directory path
   781→	debugDir := spi.GetDebugDir(sessionID)
   782→
   783→	// Create the debug directory
   784→	if err := os.MkdirAll(debugDir, 0755); err != nil {
   785→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create debug directory: %w", err)
   786→	}
   787→
   788→	// Write each record as a pretty-printed JSON file
   789→	for index, record := range records {
   790→		// Create filename with 1-based index for readability
   791→		filename := fmt.Sprintf("%d.json", index+1)
   792→		debugPath := filepath.Join(debugDir, filename)
   793→
   794→		// Pretty print the record
   795→		prettyJSON, err := json.MarshalIndent(record, "", "  ")
   796→		if err != nil {
   797→			slog.Debug("writeDebugRawFiles: Failed to marshal record to JSON",
   798→				"index", index,
   799→				"error", err)
   800→			continue
   801→		}
   802→
   803→		// Write the file
   804→		if err := os.WriteFile(debugPath, prettyJSON, 0644); err != nil {
   805→			slog.Debug("writeDebugRawFiles: Failed to write debug file",
   806→				"path", debugPath,
   807→				"error", err)
   808→			continue
   809→		}
   810→
   811→		slog.Debug("writeDebugRawFiles: Wrote debug file",
   812→			"path", debugPath,
   813→			"index", index)
   814→	}
   815→
   816→	return nil
   817→}
   818→
   819→// findFirstUserMessage extracts the first user message from Codex CLI session records.
   820→// Returns empty string if no user message is found.
   821→func findFirstUserMessage(records []map[string]interface{}) string {
   822→	for _, record := range records {
   823→		// Get record type
   824→		recordType, ok := record["type"].(string)
   825→		if !ok || recordType != "event_msg" {
   826→			continue
   827→		}
   828→
   829→		// Get payload
   830→		payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{})
   831→		if !ok {
   832→			continue
   833→		}
   834→
   835→		// Check if this is a user message
   836→		payloadType, ok := payload["type"].(string)
   837→		if !ok || payloadType != "user_message" {
   838→			continue
   839→		}
   840→
   841→		// Extract the message content
   842→		message, ok := payload["message"].(string)
   843→		if !ok || message == "" {
   844→			continue
   845→		}
   846→
   847→		// Found the first user message
   848→		slog.Debug("findFirstUserMessage: Found first user message",
   849→			"message", message[:min(len(message), 100)])
   850→		return message
   851→	}
   852→
   853→	slog.Debug("findFirstUserMessage: No user message found in session")
   854→	return ""
   855→}
   856→
   857→// ListAgentChatSessions retrieves lightweight session metadata without full parsing
   858→// This is much faster than GetAgentChatSessions as it only reads minimal data from each session
   859→func (p *Provider) ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath string) ([]spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
   860→	// Find all sessions for this project (don't stop on first)
   861→	sessions, err := findCodexSessions(projectPath, "", false)
   862→	if err != nil {
   863→		// If sessions directory doesn't exist, return empty list (not an error)
   864→		if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions directory not accessible") ||
   865→			strings.Contains(err.Error(), "sessions root") ||
   866→			strings.Contains(err.Error(), "home directory") {
   867→			return []spi.SessionMetadata{}, nil
   868→		}
   869→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to find codex sessions: %w", err)
   870→	}
   871→
   872→	// Extract metadata from each session
   873→	result := make([]spi.SessionMetadata, 0, len(sessions))
   874→	for _, sessionInfo := range sessions {
   875→		metadata, err := extractCodexSessionMetadata(&sessionInfo)
   876→		if err != nil {
   877→			slog.Warn("Failed to extract session metadata",
   878→				"sessionID", sessionInfo.SessionID,
   879→				"path", sessionInfo.SessionPath,
   880→				"error", err)
   881→			continue
   882→		}
   883→
   884→		// Skip empty sessions (no metadata means empty session)
   885→		if metadata == nil {
   886→			slog.Debug("Skipping empty session", "sessionID", sessionInfo.SessionID)
   887→			continue
   888→		}
   889→
   890→		result = append(result, *metadata)
   891→	}
   892→
   893→	return result, nil
   894→}
   895→
   896→// extractCodexSessionMetadata reads minimal data from a Codex CLI session to extract metadata
   897→// Returns nil if the session is empty or has no user messages
   898→func extractCodexSessionMetadata(sessionInfo *codexSessionInfo) (*spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
   899→	file, err := os.Open(sessionInfo.SessionPath)
   900→	if err != nil {
   901→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open session file: %w", err)
   902→	}
   903→	defer func() {
   904→		_ = file.Close()
   905→	}()
   906→
   907→	reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
   908→	var firstUserMessage string
   909→	lineNum := 0
   910→
   911→	// Read records until we find a user message or reach EOF.
   912→	// Why: ReadString can return data AND io.EOF on the last line (no trailing newline),
   913→	// so we always process the line first, then check for EOF once at the bottom.
   914→	for {
   915→		line, readErr := reader.ReadString('\n')
   916→		if readErr != nil && readErr != io.EOF {
   917→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read line: %w", readErr)
   918→		}
   919→
   920→		lineNum++
   921→		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   922→
   923→		if line != "" {
   924→			// Parse JSON record
   925→			var record map[string]interface{}
   926→			if jsonErr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); jsonErr != nil {
   927→				slog.Warn("Skipping malformed JSONL line",
   928→					"file", filepath.Base(sessionInfo.SessionPath),
   929→					"line", lineNum,
   930→					"error", jsonErr)
   931→			} else if recordType, ok := record["type"].(string); ok && recordType == "event_msg" {
   932→				if payload, ok := record["payload"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {
   933→					if payloadType, ok := payload["type"].(string); ok && payloadType == "user_message" {
   934→						if message, ok := payload["message"].(string); ok && message != "" {
   935→							firstUserMessage = message
   936→						}
   937→					}
   938→				}
   939→			}
   940→		}
   941→
   942→		// Single exit: found what we need, or reached end of file
   943→		if firstUserMessage != "" || readErr == io.EOF {
   944→			break
   945→		}
   946→	}
   947→
   948→	// If no user message found, session is empty
   949→	if firstUserMessage == "" {
   950→		return nil, nil
   951→	}
   952→
   953→	// Generate slug from first user message
   954→	slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(firstUserMessage)
   955→
   956→	// Generate human-readable name from first user message
   957→	name := spi.GenerateReadableName(firstUserMessage)
   958→
   959→	return &spi.SessionMetadata{
   960→		SessionID: sessionInfo.SessionID,
   961→		CreatedAt: sessionInfo.Meta.Timestamp,
   962→		Slug:      slug,
   963→		Name:      name,
   964→	}, nil
   965→}
   966→
```

```text
    23→// Provider implements the SPI Provider interface for Factory Droid CLI.
    24→type Provider struct{}

    26→// NewProvider creates a new Factory Droid CLI provider instance.

    35→// Check verifies that the Droid CLI is available and returns its resolved
    36→// location and version information based on the provided custom command override.
    37→func (p *Provider) Check(customCommand string) spi.CheckResult {
    38→	cmdName, _ := parseDroidCommand(customCommand)
    39→	isCustom := strings.TrimSpace(customCommand) != ""
    40→	versionFlag := "--version"
    41→
    42→	resolved, err := exec.LookPath(cmdName)
    43→	if err != nil {
    44→		msg := buildCheckErrorMessage("not_found", cmdName, isCustom, "")
    45→		trackCheckFailure("droid", isCustom, cmdName, "", classifyDroidPath(cmdName, ""), versionFlag, "", "not_found", err.Error())
    46→		return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: "", ErrorMessage: msg}
    47→	}
    48→	pathType := classifyDroidPath(cmdName, resolved)
    49→
    50→	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
    51→	cmd := exec.Command(resolved, versionFlag)
    52→	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
    53→	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
    54→	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
    55→		errorType := classifyCheckError(err)
    56→		stderrOutput := strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String())
    57→		msg := buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType, resolved, isCustom, stderrOutput)
    58→		trackCheckFailure("droid", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, pathType, versionFlag, stderrOutput, errorType, err.Error())
    59→		return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: resolved, ErrorMessage: msg}
    60→	}
    61→
    62→	versionOutput := sanitizeDroidVersion(stdout.String())
    63→	version := extractDroidVersion(versionOutput)
    64→	if version == "" {
    65→		version = strings.TrimSpace(versionOutput)
    66→	}
    67→	trackCheckSuccess("droid", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, pathType, version, versionFlag)
    68→	return spi.CheckResult{Success: true, Version: version, Location: resolved}
    69→}
    70→
    71→// DetectAgent reports whether the Factory Droid CLI agent is relevant for the given
    72→// projectPath based on existing session files. When helpOutput is true, it may log
    73→// warnings or print detection help to guide the user if detection fails.
    74→func (p *Provider) DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool {
    75→	files, err := listSessionFiles()
    76→	if err != nil {
    77→		if helpOutput {
    78→			log.UserWarn("Factory Droid detection failed: %v", err)
    79→		}
    80→		return false
    81→	}
    82→	if len(files) == 0 {
    83→		if helpOutput {
    84→			printDetectionHelp()
    85→		}
    86→		return false
    87→	}
    88→	if projectPath == "" {
    89→		return true
    90→	}
    91→	for _, file := range files {
    92→		if sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
    93→			return true
    94→		}
    95→	}
    96→	if helpOutput {
    97→		printDetectionHelp()
    98→	}
    99→	return false
   100→}
   101→
   102→// GetAgentChatSessions returns all available agent chat sessions from the Factory Droid CLI,
   103→// optionally filtered to sessions associated with the given projectPath. When debugRaw is true,
   104→// additional raw session details may be included in the converted sessions.
   105→func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
   106→	files, err := listSessionFiles()
   107→	if err != nil {
   108→		return nil, err
   109→	}
   110→	result := make([]spi.AgentChatSession, 0, len(files))
   111→	normalizedProject := strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
   112→
   113→	// Filter files to those matching the project (for accurate progress count)
   114→	var matchingFiles []sessionFile
   115→	for _, file := range files {
   116→		if normalizedProject != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, normalizedProject) {
   117→			continue
   118→		}
   119→		matchingFiles = append(matchingFiles, file)
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	totalFiles := len(matchingFiles)
   123→
   124→	for i, file := range matchingFiles {
   125→		session, err := parseFactorySession(file.Path)
   126→		if err != nil {
   127→			slog.Debug("droidcli: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
   128→			// Report progress even for failed sessions
   129→			if progress != nil {
   130→				progress(i+1, totalFiles)
   131→			}
   132→			continue
   133→		}
   134→		chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
   135→		if chat != nil {
   136→			result = append(result, *chat)
   137→		}
   138→
   139→		// Report progress after each session
   140→		if progress != nil {
   141→			progress(i+1, totalFiles)
   142→		}
   143→	}
   144→	return result, nil
   145→}
   146→
   147→// GetAgentChatSession returns the agent chat session with the given ID if it exists
   148→// and is associated with the provided project path. It returns (nil, nil) if the
   149→// session is found but does not match the project filter.
   150→func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
   151→	path, err := findSessionFileByID(sessionID)
   152→	if err != nil || path == "" {
   153→		return nil, err
   154→	}
   155→	if strings.TrimSpace(projectPath) != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(path, projectPath) {
   156→		return nil, nil
   157→	}
   158→	session, err := parseFactorySession(path)
   159→	if err != nil {
   160→		return nil, err
   161→	}
   162→	return convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw), nil
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ExecAgentAndWatch executes the Factory Droid CLI agent for the given project and,
   166→// when a session callback is provided, watches for new or updated chat sessions and
   167→// invokes the callback as they are discovered.
   168→func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
   169→	if sessionCallback == nil {
   170→		return ExecuteDroid(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
   171→	}
   172→
   173→	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
   174→	defer cancel()
   175→
   176→	watchErr := make(chan error, 1)
   177→	go func() {
   178→		watchErr <- watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
   179→	}()
   180→
   181→	err := ExecuteDroid(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
   182→	cancel()
   183→
   184→	if werr := <-watchErr; werr != nil && !errors.Is(werr, context.Canceled) {
   185→		slog.Warn("droidcli: watcher stopped with error", "error", werr)
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	if err != nil {
   189→		return fmt.Errorf("factory droid CLI execution failed: %w", err)
   190→	}
   191→	return nil
   192→}
   193→
   194→// WatchAgent monitors agent chat sessions for the given project and invokes
   195→// sessionCallback for each new or updated session until ctx is canceled.
   196→func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
   197→	if sessionCallback == nil {
   198→		return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
   199→	}
   200→	return watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
   201→}
   202→
   203→func convertToAgentSession(session *fdSession, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
   204→	if session == nil {
   205→		return nil
   206→	}
   207→	if len(session.Blocks) == 0 {
   208→		return nil
   209→	}
   210→	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(session, workspaceRoot)
   211→	if err != nil {
   212→		slog.Debug("droidcli: skipping session due to conversion error", "sessionId", session.ID, "error", err)
   213→		return nil
   214→	}
   215→	created := strings.TrimSpace(sessionData.CreatedAt)
   216→	chat := &spi.AgentChatSession{
   217→		SessionID:   session.ID,
   218→		CreatedAt:   created,
   219→		Slug:        session.Slug,
   220→		SessionData: sessionData,
   221→		RawData:     session.RawData,
   222→	}
   223→	if debugRaw {
   224→		if err := writeFactoryDebugRaw(session); err != nil {
   225→			slog.Debug("droidcli: debug raw failed", "sessionId", session.ID, "error", err)
   226→		}
   227→	}
   228→	return chat
   229→}
   230→
   231→func classifyCheckError(err error) string {
   232→	var execErr *exec.Error
   233→	var pathErr *os.PathError
   234→	switch {
   235→	case errors.As(err, &execErr) && execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound:
   236→		return "not_found"
   237→	case errors.As(err, &pathErr) && errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrPermission):
   238→		return "permission_denied"
   239→	case errors.Is(err, os.ErrPermission):
   240→		return "permission_denied"
   241→	default:
   242→		return "version_failed"
   243→	}
   244→}
   245→
   246→func buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType string, command string, isCustom bool, stderr string) string {
   247→	var builder strings.Builder
   248→	switch errorType {
   249→	case "not_found":
   250→		builder.WriteString("Factory Droid CLI was not found.\n\n")
   251→		if isCustom {
   252→			builder.WriteString("• Verify the custom path you provided is executable.\n")
   253→			builder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("• Provided command: %s\n", command))
   254→		} else {
   255→			builder.WriteString("• Install the Factory CLI and ensure `droid` is on your PATH.\n")
   256→			builder.WriteString("• Re-run `specstory check droid` after installation.\n")
   257→		}
   258→	case "permission_denied":
   259→		builder.WriteString("SpecStory cannot execute the Factory CLI due to permissions.\n\n")
   260→		builder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("Try: chmod +x %s\n", command))
   261→	default:
   262→		builder.WriteString("`droid --version` failed.\n\n")
   263→		if stderr != "" {
   264→			builder.WriteString("Error output:\n")
   265→			builder.WriteString(stderr)
   266→			builder.WriteString("\n\n")
   267→		}
   268→		builder.WriteString("Run `droid --version` manually to diagnose, then retry.")
   269→	}
   270→	return builder.String()
   271→}
   272→
   273→var ansiRegexp = regexp.MustCompile(`\x1b\[[0-9;]*[A-Za-z]`)
   274→
   275→func sanitizeDroidVersion(raw string) string {
   276→	clean := strings.ReplaceAll(raw, "\r", "")
   277→	clean = ansiRegexp.ReplaceAllString(clean, "")
   278→	return clean
   279→}
   280→
   281→func extractDroidVersion(raw string) string {
   282→	lines := strings.Split(raw, "\n")
   283→	var filtered []string
   284→	for _, line := range lines {
   285→		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   286→		if line == "" {
   287→			continue
   288→		}
   289→		filtered = append(filtered, line)
   290→	}
   291→	if len(filtered) == 0 {
   292→		return ""
   293→	}
   294→	return filtered[len(filtered)-1]
   295→}
   296→
   297→func trackCheckSuccess(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, pathType string, version string, versionFlag string) {
   298→	props := analytics.Properties{
   299→		"provider":       provider,
   300→		"custom_command": custom,
   301→		"command_path":   commandPath,
   302→		"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
   303→		"path_type":      pathType,
   304→		"version":        version,
   305→		"version_flag":   versionFlag,
   306→	}
   307→	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallSuccess, props)
   308→}
   309→
   310→func trackCheckFailure(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, pathType string, versionFlag string, stderrOutput string, errorType string, message string) {
   311→	props := analytics.Properties{
   312→		"provider":       provider,
   313→		"custom_command": custom,
   314→		"command_path":   commandPath,
   315→		"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
   316→		"path_type":      pathType,
   317→		"version_flag":   versionFlag,
   318→		"error_type":     errorType,
   319→		"error_message":  message,
   320→	}
   321→	if stderrOutput != "" {
   322→		props["stderr"] = stderrOutput
   323→	}
   324→	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, props)
   325→}
   326→
   327→func classifyDroidPath(command string, resolvedPath string) string {
   328→	if resolvedPath == "" {
   329→		if filepath.IsAbs(command) {
   330→			return "absolute_path"
   331→		}
   332→		return "unknown"
   333→	}
   334→	resolvedLower := strings.ToLower(resolvedPath)
   335→	if strings.Contains(resolvedLower, "homebrew") || strings.Contains(resolvedLower, "/opt/homebrew/") {
   336→		return "homebrew"
   337→	}
   338→	if strings.Contains(resolvedLower, "/.local/bin/") {
   339→		return "user_local"
   340→	}
   341→	if strings.Contains(resolvedLower, "/.factory/") {
   342→		return "factory_local"
   343→	}
   344→	if filepath.IsAbs(command) {
   345→		return "absolute_path"
   346→	}
   347→	return "system_path"
   348→}
   349→
   350→func printDetectionHelp() {
   351→	log.UserMessage("No Factory Droid sessions found under ~/.factory/sessions yet.\n")
   352→	log.UserMessage("Run the Factory CLI inside this project to create a JSONL session, then rerun `specstory sync droid`.\n")
   353→}
   354→
   355→func sessionMentionsProject(filePath string, projectPath string) bool {
   356→	projectPath = strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
   357→	if projectPath == "" {
   358→		return false
   359→	}
   360→
   361→	canonicalProject := canonicalizePath(projectPath)
   362→	if sessionRoot := extractSessionWorkspaceRoot(filePath); sessionRoot != "" {
   363→		canonicalRoot := canonicalizePath(sessionRoot)
   364→		if canonicalProject != "" && canonicalRoot != "" {
   365→			return canonicalRoot == canonicalProject
   366→		}
   367→		return strings.TrimSpace(sessionRoot) == projectPath
   368→	}
   369→
   370→	return sessionMentionsProjectText(filePath, projectPath)
   371→}
   372→
   373→func extractSessionWorkspaceRoot(filePath string) string {
   374→	file, err := os.Open(filePath)
   375→	if err != nil {
   376→		return ""
   377→	}
   378→	defer func() {
   379→		_ = file.Close()
   380→	}()
   381→
   382→	var workspaceRoot string
   383→	scanErr := scanLines(file, func(_ int, line string) error {
   384→		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
   385→		if trimmed == "" {
   386→			return nil
   387→		}
   388→		var env jsonlEnvelope
   389→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &env); err != nil {
   390→			return nil
   391→		}
   392→		if env.Type != "session_start" {
   393→			return nil
   394→		}
   395→		var event sessionStartEvent
   396→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &event); err != nil {
   397→			return nil
   398→		}
   399→		workspaceRoot = firstNonEmpty(event.CWD, event.WorkspaceRoot, event.Session.CWD, event.Session.WorkspaceRoot)
   400→		return errStopScan
   401→	})
   402→	if scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, errStopScan) {
   403→		return ""
   404→	}
   405→
   406→	return strings.TrimSpace(workspaceRoot)
   407→}
   408→
   409→func canonicalizePath(path string) string {
   410→	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(path)
   411→	if trimmed == "" {
   412→		return ""
   413→	}
   414→	canonical, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(trimmed)
   415→	if err == nil {
   416→		return canonical
   417→	}
   418→	if abs, absErr := filepath.Abs(trimmed); absErr == nil {
   419→		return filepath.Clean(abs)
   420→	}
   421→	return filepath.Clean(trimmed)
   422→}
   423→
   424→func sessionMentionsProjectText(filePath string, projectPath string) bool {
   425→	file, err := os.Open(filePath)
   426→	if err != nil {
   427→		return false
   428→	}
   429→	defer func() {
   430→		_ = file.Close()
   431→	}()
   432→	needle := strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
   433→	short := filepath.Base(projectPath)
   434→	foundLines := 0
   435→	err = scanLines(file, func(_ int, line string) error {
   436→		if needle != "" && strings.Contains(line, needle) {
   437→			return errStopScan
   438→		}
   439→		if short != "" && strings.Contains(line, short) {
   440→			foundLines++
   441→			if foundLines > 2 {
   442→				return errStopScan
   443→			}
   444→		}
   445→		return nil
   446→	})
   447→
   448→	if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, errStopScan) {
   449→		slog.Warn("error scanning session file", "path", filePath, "err", err)
   450→		return false
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	return errors.Is(err, errStopScan)
   454→}
   455→
   456→// writeFactoryDebugRaw writes pretty-printed JSON files for each line in the session's JSONL.
   457→// Each JSONL line becomes a numbered file (1.json, 2.json, etc.) matching the Claude Code provider format.
   458→func writeFactoryDebugRaw(session *fdSession) error {
   459→	if session == nil {
   460→		return nil
   461→	}
   462→
   463→	dir := spi.GetDebugDir(session.ID)
   464→	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
   465→		return fmt.Errorf("droidcli: unable to create debug dir: %w", err)
   466→	}
   467→
   468→	// Clean existing files from the debug directory
   469→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
   470→	if err == nil {
   471→		for _, entry := range entries {
   472→			if !entry.IsDir() {
   473→				_ = os.Remove(filepath.Join(dir, entry.Name()))
   474→			}
   475→		}
   476→	}
   477→
   478→	// Split JSONL into individual lines and write each as a pretty-printed JSON file
   479→	lines := strings.Split(session.RawData, "\n")
   480→	lineNumber := 0
   481→	for _, line := range lines {
   482→		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
   483→		if trimmed == "" {
   484→			continue
   485→		}
   486→
   487→		// Parse the JSON line
   488→		var data map[string]interface{}
   489→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(trimmed), &data); err != nil {
   490→			slog.Debug("droidcli: skipping malformed JSONL line in debug-raw",
   491→				"lineNumber", lineNumber+1,
   492→				"error", err)
   493→			continue
   494→		}
   495→
   496→		lineNumber++
   497→
   498→		// Pretty-print and write to numbered file
   499→		prettyJSON, err := json.MarshalIndent(data, "", "  ")
   500→		if err != nil {
   501→			slog.Debug("droidcli: failed to marshal JSON for debug-raw",
   502→				"lineNumber", lineNumber,
   503→				"error", err)
   504→			continue
   505→		}
   506→
   507→		filePath := filepath.Join(dir, fmt.Sprintf("%d.json", lineNumber))
   508→		if err := os.WriteFile(filePath, prettyJSON, 0o644); err != nil {
   509→			slog.Debug("droidcli: failed to write debug-raw file",
   510→				"path", filePath,
   511→				"error", err)
   512→		}
   513→	}
   514→
   515→	slog.Debug("droidcli: wrote debug-raw files",
   516→		"sessionId", session.ID,
   517→		"fileCount", lineNumber)
   518→
   519→	return nil
   520→}
   521→
   522→// ListAgentChatSessions retrieves lightweight session metadata without full parsing
   523→func (p *Provider) ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath string) ([]spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
   524→	files, err := listSessionFiles()
   525→	if err != nil {
   526→		return nil, err
   527→	}
   528→
   529→	normalizedProject := strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
   530→
   531→	// Filter files to those matching the project
   532→	var matchingFiles []sessionFile
   533→	for _, file := range files {
   534→		if normalizedProject != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, normalizedProject) {
   535→			continue
   536→		}
   537→		matchingFiles = append(matchingFiles, file)
   538→	}
   539→
   540→	// Extract metadata from each session
   541→	result := make([]spi.SessionMetadata, 0, len(matchingFiles))
   542→	for _, file := range matchingFiles {
   543→		metadata, err := extractDroidSessionMetadata(file.Path)
   544→		if err != nil {
   545→			slog.Debug("Failed to extract session metadata",
   546→				"path", file.Path,
   547→				"error", err)
   548→			continue
   549→		}
   550→
   551→		// Skip empty sessions
   552→		if metadata == nil {
   553→			slog.Debug("Skipping empty session", "path", file.Path)
   554→			continue
   555→		}
   556→
   557→		result = append(result, *metadata)
   558→	}
   559→
   560→	return result, nil
   561→}
   562→
   563→// extractDroidSessionMetadata extracts lightweight metadata from a Droid session file
   564→// Returns nil if the session is empty or has no blocks
   565→func extractDroidSessionMetadata(filePath string) (*spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
   566→	session, err := parseFactorySession(filePath)
   567→	if err != nil {
   568→		return nil, err
   569→	}
   570→
   571→	// Skip sessions with no blocks
   572→	if session == nil || len(session.Blocks) == 0 {
   573→		return nil, nil
   574→	}
   575→
   576→	// Generate name from title or first user message
   577→	name := generateDroidSessionName(session)
   578→
   579→	return &spi.SessionMetadata{
   580→		SessionID: session.ID,
   581→		CreatedAt: session.CreatedAt,
   582→		Slug:      session.Slug,
   583→		Name:      name,
   584→	}, nil
   585→}
   586→
   587→// generateDroidSessionName generates a human-readable name for a Droid session
   588→// Prefers the Title field if available, otherwise uses the first user message
   589→func generateDroidSessionName(session *fdSession) string {
   590→	// Use title if available (already human-readable)
   591→	if title := strings.TrimSpace(session.Title); title != "" {
   592→		// Apply the same length limit as other providers for consistency
   593→		return spi.GenerateReadableName(title)
   594→	}
   595→
   596→	// Fall back to first user message
   597→	for _, block := range session.Blocks {
   598→		if block.Role == "user" {
   599→			text := strings.TrimSpace(block.Text)
   600→			if text != "" {
   601→				return spi.GenerateReadableName(text)
   602→			}
   603→		}
   604→	}
   605→
   606→	// No suitable content found
   607→	return ""
   608→}
   609→
```

```text
    24→
    25→// getDefaultCursorCommand returns the default cursor-agent command.
    26→// It checks for cursor-agent in common locations in this order:
    27→// 1. cursor-agent in PATH
    28→// 2. ~/.cursor/bin/cursor-agent
    29→// 3. ~/.local/bin/cursor-agent
    30→// This allows flexibility in installation locations.
    31→func getDefaultCursorCommand() string {
    32→	// Check if cursor-agent is in PATH first
    33→	if _, err := execLookPath("cursor-agent"); err == nil {
    34→		slog.Info("Found cursor-agent in PATH")
    35→		return "cursor-agent"
    36→	}
    37→
    38→	// Check common installation locations
    39→	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
    40→	if err == nil {
    41→		// Check ~/.cursor/bin/cursor-agent
    42→		cursorInstallPath := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".cursor", "bin", "cursor-agent")
    43→		if _, err := osStat(cursorInstallPath); err == nil {
    44→			slog.Info("Found cursor-agent", "path", cursorInstallPath)
    45→			return cursorInstallPath
    46→		}
    47→
    48→		// Check ~/.local/bin/cursor-agent
    49→		userLocalPath := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".local", "bin", "cursor-agent")
    50→		if _, err := osStat(userLocalPath); err == nil {
    51→			slog.Info("Found cursor-agent", "path", userLocalPath)
    52→			return userLocalPath
    53→		}
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	// Default to cursor-agent and hope it's in PATH
    57→	slog.Info("Using cursor-agent from PATH (may not exist)")
    58→	return "cursor-agent"
    59→}
    60→
    61→// expandTilde expands ~ to the user's home directory
    62→func expandTilde(path string) string {
    63→	if strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {
    64→		homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
    65→		if err == nil {
    66→			return filepath.Join(homeDir, path[2:])
    67→		}
    68→	}
    69→	return path
    70→}
    71→
    72→// getPathType determines the type of path being used for Cursor CLI.
    73→// Returns one of the following for analytics tracking:
    74→//   - "cursor_bin": Cursor's standard installation directory (~/.cursor/bin/)
    75→//   - "user_local": User's local bin directory (~/.local/bin/)
    76→//   - "absolute_path": Custom absolute path provided by user
    77→//   - "system_path": Found in system PATH environment variable
    78→//
    79→// This helps understand how users have Cursor CLI installed for support and usage patterns.
    80→func getPathType(cursorCmd, resolvedPath string) string {
    81→	if strings.Contains(resolvedPath, "/.cursor/bin/") {
    82→		return "cursor_bin"
    83→	} else if strings.Contains(resolvedPath, "/.local/bin/") {
    84→		return "user_local"
    85→	} else if filepath.IsAbs(cursorCmd) {
    86→		return "absolute_path"
    87→	} else {
    88→		return "system_path"
    89→	}
    90→}
    91→
    92→// parseCursorCommand parses a custom command string into executable and arguments.
    93→// If customCommand is empty, returns the default command.
    94→// Supports quoted strings with spaces: cursor-agent --arg "value with spaces"
    95→// Example: "cursor-agent --model gpt-4" returns ("cursor-agent", ["--model", "gpt-4"])
    96→func parseCursorCommand(customCommand string) (string, []string) {
    97→	if customCommand != "" {
    98→		parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
    99→		if len(parts) > 0 {
   100→			// Expand tilde in the command path
   101→			return expandTilde(parts[0]), parts[1:]
   102→		}
   103→	}
   104→	return getDefaultCursorCommand(), nil
   105→}
   106→
   107→// ExecuteCursorCLI runs the Cursor CLI with the given arguments and optional resume session
   108→func ExecuteCursorCLI(customCommand string, resumeSessionId string) error {
   109→	slog.Info("Preparing to execute Cursor CLI", "command", customCommand, "resumeSessionId", resumeSessionId)
   110→
   111→	// Parse the command and any custom arguments
   112→	cursorCmd, customArgs := parseCursorCommand(customCommand)
   113→
   114→	// Add --resume flag if sessionId is provided
   115→	if resumeSessionId != "" {
   116→		customArgs = append(customArgs, "--resume", resumeSessionId)
   117→		slog.Info("ExecuteCursorCLI: Resuming session", "sessionId", resumeSessionId)
   118→	}
   119→
   120→	// Create the command
   121→	cmd := exec.Command(cursorCmd, customArgs...)
   122→
   123→	// Set up stdin/stdout/stderr to match the parent process
   124→	cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
   125→	cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
   126→	cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
   127→
   128→	// Ensure cursor-agent runs with proper terminal settings
   129→	cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "FORCE_COLOR=1")
   130→
   131→	// Start the command
   132→	slog.Info("ExecuteCursorCLI: Starting Cursor CLI process", "command", cursorCmd, "args", customArgs)
   133→	if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
   134→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to start cursor-agent: %w", err)
   135→	}
   136→
   137→	slog.Info("ExecuteCursorCLI: Cursor CLI started", "pid", cmd.Process.Pid)
   138→
   139→	// Set up signal handling to properly terminate the child process
   140→	sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
   141→	signal.Notify(sigChan, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
   142→	defer signal.Stop(sigChan)
   143→
   144→	// Wait for the command to finish or for a signal
   145→	done := make(chan error, 1)
   146→	go func() {
   147→		done <- cmd.Wait()
   148→	}()
   149→
   150→	select {
   151→	case err := <-done:
   152→		if err != nil {
   153→			// Don't return error if the command exited with a non-zero status
   154→			// This is normal for many CLI applications
   155→			if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
   156→				exitCode := exitErr.ExitCode()
   157→				slog.Info("ExecuteCursorCLI: Cursor CLI exited", "exitCode", exitCode)
   158→				os.Exit(exitCode)
   159→			}
   160→			slog.Error("ExecuteCursorCLI: Cursor CLI exited with error", "error", err)
   161→			return fmt.Errorf("cursor-agent execution failed: %w", err)
   162→		}
   163→		slog.Info("ExecuteCursorCLI: Cursor CLI exited normally", "exitCode", 0)
   164→		return nil
   165→	case sig := <-sigChan:
   166→		slog.Info("ExecuteCursorCLI: Received signal, terminating Cursor CLI", "signal", sig)
   167→		// Give the process a chance to clean up
   168→		if err := cmd.Process.Signal(os.Interrupt); err != nil {
   169→			slog.Error("ExecuteCursorCLI: Failed to send interrupt signal", "error", err)
   170→			// If interrupt fails, force kill
   171→			if err := cmd.Process.Kill(); err != nil {
   172→				slog.Error("ExecuteCursorCLI: Failed to kill process", "error", err)
   173→			}
   174→		}
   175→		// Wait a bit to allow Cursor CLI a clean shutdown from the interrupt signal we sent
   176→		time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
   177→		return fmt.Errorf("process interrupted by signal: %v", sig)
   178→	}
   179→}
   180→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
   281→func extractDroidVersion(raw string) string {
   282→	lines := strings.Split(raw, "\n")
   283→	var filtered []string
   284→	for _, line := range lines {
   285→		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   286→		if line == "" {
   287→			continue
   288→		}
   289→		filtered = append(filtered, line)
   290→	}
   291→	if len(filtered) == 0 {
   292→		return ""
   293→	}
   294→	return filtered[len(filtered)-1]
   295→}
```

## Summary Report: `getPathType` and `path_type` Occurrences

I've found all occurrences of `getPathType` and `path_type` in the Go codebase. Here's the complete breakdown:

### 1. **getPathType function definition and usage**

**File:** `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/cursor_cli_exec.go`
- **Lines 72-90:** Function definition
  - Determines the type of path being used for Cursor CLI
  - Returns one of: "cursor_bin", "user_local", "absolute_path", or "system_path"
  - Used for analytics tracking to understand how users have Cursor CLI installed

```go
func getPathType(cursorCmd, resolvedPath string) string {
	if strings.Contains(resolvedPath, "/.cursor/bin/") {
		return "cursor_bin"
	} else if strings.Contains(resolvedPath, "/.local/bin/") {
		return "user_local"
	} else if filepath.IsAbs(cursorCmd) {
		return "absolute_path"
	} else {
		return "system_path"
	}
}
```

**File:** `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/cursorcli/provider.go`

```text
-## v1.7.0 2026-02-12

 ### ⚙️ Improvements

+	homeDir, homeDirErr := osUserHomeDir()
+	if homeDirErr == nil {
+		// Preferred native installation
+		nativeClaude := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".local", "bin", "claude")
+		if _, err := osStat(nativeClaude); err == nil {
+			slog.Info("Found Claude Code", "path", nativeClaude)
+			return nativeClaude
 		}
+	}
+
+	// Check if claude is available on PATH

+	if homeDirErr == nil {
+		// Legacy npm installation
+		legacyClaude := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude", "local", "claude")
+		if _, err := osStat(legacyClaude); err == nil {
+			slog.Info("Found Claude Code", "path", legacyClaude)
+			return legacyClaude
 		}
 	}
-	slog.Info("Using Claude Code from PATH")
+
+	slog.Info("Using Claude Code bare fallback")

 }
```

```text
fd493b8 Don't refresh expired access tokens while blocking on startup of `specstory run`. ```
```
```
Prefer native Claude Code install over npm; remove path_type analytics from all providers.

Reorder getDefaultClaudeCommand to check ~/.local/bin/claude (native) before
~/.claude/local/claude (legacy npm), with an explicit PATH check in between.
Remove getPathType/classifyCodexPath/classifyDroidPath and the path_type
analytics property across all four providers for consistency. Improve
readability by renaming ambiguous err variable to homeDirErr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```
